payload.rationale: The pairing keeps geometry authoring and mesh generation explicit, scriptable, and directly tied to the verified geometry runtime.
payload.statement: Default to CadQuery for parametric CAD and Gmsh for meshing in the implemented runtime-linked set.
payload.title: Use CadQuery plus Gmsh for scripted geometry-to-mesh flow
tradeoffs: Requires explicit translation and mesh-quality checks at the handoff boundary

payload.rationale: Each tool stays inside the use case it is strongest at while sharing one verified thermochemistry runtime profile.
payload.statement: Use Cantera for chemistry, CoolProp for properties, and TESPy for thermal-system networks in the implemented stack.
payload.title: Use Cantera, CoolProp, and TESPy for thermochemistry and system thermals
tradeoffs: Requires explicit data contracts between chemistry, property, and system layers

payload.rationale: An auditable model graph is safer than accumulating implicit shell-script orchestration.
payload.statement: Use OpenMDAO when multiple implemented discipline adapters need one explicit workflow graph.
payload.title: Prefer OpenMDAO over ad hoc orchestration
tradeoffs: Higher wrapper cost up front but much clearer discipline boundaries

payload.rationale: The implemented MDO runtime includes both, but their use cases differ materially and should not be conflated.
payload.statement: Use CasADi for differentiable graph-based optimization and pymoo for black-box multi-objective search.
payload.title: Split differentiable and black-box optimization between CasADi and pymoo
tradeoffs: Teams need to choose the optimization mode explicitly per problem class

payload.rationale: Translation should be a declared artifact boundary, not an opaque side effect.
payload.statement: Use meshio when the implemented stack crosses mesh or field file formats.
payload.title: Use meshio for explicit mesh-format translation
tradeoffs: Adds one explicit translation step but makes downstream debugging much easier

payload.rationale: Dimensional consistency should be enforced in the runtime, not left to prose conventions.
payload.statement: Default to Pint and unyt in Python flows and UnitsNet in .NET flows for implemented units discipline.
payload.title: Use Pint, unyt, and UnitsNet for units discipline
tradeoffs: Teams must normalize unit policies before moving data across runtimes

payload.rationale: These tools complement each other and share one verified MDO runtime profile.
payload.statement: Use OpenTURNS for probabilistic modeling, SMT for surrogate building, and SALib for sensitivity analysis in the implemented set.
payload.title: Use OpenTURNS, SMT, and SALib for UQ and surrogate support
tradeoffs: Requires explicit ownership of UQ versus optimization responsibilities
